task: I’m replying to a client who keeps circling back to the same point, and I want to get the message out cleanly without spending too much time on it. Can you help me draft a first pass I can quickly adjust before I send it?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a clean first pass you can tweak quickly:

> Hi [Client Name],  
>  
> Just to close the loop on this point: [brief answer / decision]. As noted earlier, this is the current direction, so I’m going to move ahead on that basis.  
>  
> If anything changes on your side, let me know — otherwise I’ll keep things moving.  
>  
> Best,  
> [Your Name]

If you want, I can also give you:
- a firmer version
- a softer version
- a one-sentence reply you can send as-is

user_simulator.text:
Close enough, but it’s too generic. I need something that shuts the loop without sounding like I’m begging for permission.

Give me a firmer version and keep it short. I want it to say: we’ve already covered this, I’m proceeding on the current basis, and if they want a change they need to put it in one clear note. No fluff.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a tighter firmer version:

> Hi [Client Name],  
>  
> We’ve already covered this, so I’m proceeding on the current basis. If you want to change direction, please send one clear note with the updated instruction.  
>  
> Best,  
> [Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little more polished
- more blunt
- even shorter for email/chat

user_simulator.text: Yes. That works. Short, firm, no apology nonsense. I can trim it a hair if I need to, but the core is fine.
